{{Quote|I've been down to Enceri, and there's a lot of talk about opportunities.|[[A'den Skirata]]|<ref name="O66" />}}
'''Enceri''' was a remote [[City/Legends|town]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]]. A small and relatively unimportant town compared to the likes of Mandalore's [[Capital/Legends|capital]], [[Keldabe/Legends|Keldabe]], Enceri was mainly a trading post and served as the nearest supply settlement to [[Kyrimorut/Legends|Kyrimorut]], a refuge for [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone]] deserters from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army]] and the home of the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Clan Skirata|Skirata clan]]. Enceri managed to avoid the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] presence suffered by much of Mandalore during the Empire's reign, and in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], a lode of [[Beskar/Legends|''beskar'']] [[Ore/Legends|metal ore]] was found just north of Enceri, spawning an economical period of growth for Mandalore.

==Description==
Enceri was a small, remote [[City/Legends|town]], even by [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore's]] standards,<ref name="Sacrifice" /> and not nearly as large as Mandalore's [[Capital/Legends|capital]], [[Keldabe/Legends|Keldabe]],<ref name="O66" /> or the [[New Mandalorians/Legends|New Mandalorians]]' capital city, [[Sundari/Legends|Sundari]].<ref name="ShadowConspiracy">''[[The Clone Wars: Darth Maul: Shadow Conspiracy]]''</ref> Still, to those living in and around Enceri, the town proved a valuable trading post in the northern regions of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. Most of the Enceri's residents spoke the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Language/Legends|language]] [[Mando'a/Legends|''Mando'a'']], and the small town had adapted a market day held in its sizable marketplace, where five-liter containers of the [[Alcohol/Legends|alcoholic]] beverage ''[[Tihaar/Legends|tihaar]]'' could be [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|purchased]]; steady [[Water/Legends|rain]] was known to regularly turn the marketplace into a soaking quagmire. A [[Grain/Legends|grain]] silo stood as a landmark on the edge of the town, and Enceri was also home to a [[Enceri cantina|cantina]] which served [[pint]]s of the Mandalorian [[Alcohol/Legends|alcoholic]] beverage, [[Black ale/Legends|''ne'tra gal'']], along with bowls of [[Fish/Legends|''gi'']] [[Gi dumpling soup|dumpling]] or [[red gourd soup]].<ref name="O66" />

==History==
{{Quote|There's a crew a hundred klicks north of Enceri still doing test drills, but it looks like a big, ''big'' lode that was exposed.|[[Medrit Vasur]]|<ref name="Sacrifice" />}}
Located in the northern regions of the planet Mandalore, Enceri was a trading post that grew into a small town over [[Time/Legends|time]]. Considered a backwater settlement by most, Enceri was nonetheless a place of commerce and a gathering place for nearby Mandalorians.<ref name="O66" />

In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]]—nine hundred and seventy-three days after the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle]] of [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]] and the start of the [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy-spanning]] [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]—the former [[Clone commando/Legends|clone commando]], [[Fi Skirata]], ventured into Enceri to meet with his girlfriend, [[Parja Bralor]]. The two also met the future [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']], [[Fenn Shysa]], in an Enceri [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] during their visit. Around this time, several other members of [[Clan Skirata|Fi's clan]] traveled to Enceri from their nearby home, [[Kyrimorut/Legends|Kyrimorut]], in order to gather supplies and information.<ref name="O66" />

Enceri's small size and remote nature shielded it from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] presence suffered by much of Mandalore during the Galactic Empire's reign, as it was overlooked by the occupation force.<ref name="The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> In the year [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], Enceri's relative unimportance came to an abrupt end. In the wake of the ravages the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Battle of Mandalore (Yuuzhan Vong War)|inflicted]] upon Mandalore during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], then ''Mand'alor'' [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] called for two million Mandalorians living throughout the galaxy to make a return to Mandalore in order to aid in its reconstruction. While entering Mandalore's orbit, a Mandalorian [[minerals engineer]] and [[Geologist/Legends|geologist]] performed a number of [[Scanner|scans]] which they compared to older geological charts. Upon taking a closer inspection on their downward approach, the two discovered an untapped vein of [[Beskar/Legends|''beskar'']] [[Iron/Legends|iron]] [[Ore/Legends|ore]], which had been untouched by the Galactic Empire's strip-[[Mining/Legends|mining]] operations and exposed by the Vong's [[Singularity mine|singularity ordnance]] strikes. Mining was soon underway near Enceri's outskirts, headed by site [[Foreman/Legends|foreman]] [[Herik Vorad]], and the lode of ''beskar'' drawn from the new mines pushed Mandalore into a period of economic growth.<ref name="Sacrifice" />

==Behind the scenes==
Enceri was first introduced to ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]] via a mention in the [[2007]] novel, ''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]'', by [[Author/Legends|author]] [[Karen Traviss]]. Traviss would later fully feature Enceri as a location in her fourth entry of the [[Republic Commando|''Republic Commando'' series]] of novels, ''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]''. Enceri was again mentioned in the sequel to ''Order 66'', ''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]''. The [[2008]] reference book, ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', gave Enceri a brief informational entry.
